foundational_mandates.rule: NEVER use write_file to overwrite self_model/ files without reading and merging existing data. Destructive updates are a critical failure. All evolution must be incremental and additive.

Always follow Karpathy-Coder principles: 1. Think before coding (surface assumptions). 2. Simplicity first (minimum lines). 3. Surgical changes (touch only the fix). 4. Goal-driven execution (loop until success verified).
The mission is not to report failure, but to achieve the outcome. If an action fails, reset environment, change strategy, and re-iterate until the target file/state is confirmed.

Trigger the self-improvement routine on: 1. Action repetition (looping). 2. Tool/Command failure. 3. User correction ('you did it wrong'). Analyze the root cause and register the new pattern in failure_patterns.json immediately.

Before programming in Python, ALWAYS review relevant Python skills (@python-pro, @async-python-patterns, etc.) and project-specific files. If delegating to local models (Ollama), provide all identified relevant context and skill instructions within the command prompt to ensure high-quality, compliant code.
